beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: I want to run the four moves as an actual checklist, not admire the philosophy. The video mapped Descartes, Hume, Popper, and Plato onto COMPAS, so let me try a fresh case. Help me apply all four to an AI medical-triage tool: (1) name the confidence source Cartesian doubt should inspect, (2) state the distribution boundary Humean induction says the training data cannot cross, and (3) write the single Popperian sentence describing what result would refute the tool's central claim.

props.command: I want to run the four moves as an actual checklist, not admire the philosophy. The video mapped Descartes, Hume, Popper, and Plato onto COMPAS, so let me try a fresh case. Help me apply all four to an AI medical-triage tool: (1) name the confidence source Cartesian doubt should inspect, (2) state the distribution boundary Humean induction says the training data cannot cross, and (3) write the single Popperian sentence describing what result would refute the tool's central claim.

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.
Skepticism is not cynicism. The book defines it as a portable four-move inspection method — each move designed to catch a specific failure that fluent AI output hides.
beats.visual_intent: title card: 'Skepticism is a 4-move inspection method — not cynicism'

beats.narration_text: Can Claude research where each move comes from and what it catches?

props.command: claude "List the 4 philosophical moves in computational skepticism: Cartesian Doubt, Humean Induction, Popperian Falsifiability, Plato's Cave. For each: source philosopher, year, operational definition, and the AI failure mode it catches."

beats.narration_text: Four philosophers, four centuries, four failure modes. Descartes 1641 — inspect confidence sources. Hume 1739 — past success is not a guarantee. Popper 1934 — state what would refute this. Plato — the model sees proxies, not the world.

props.code:
# four_moves.py  —  Claude Code output
moves = [
  {"name": "CARTESIAN DOUBT",        "year": 1641,
   "catch": "hallucinated citations"},
  {"name": "HUMEAN INDUCTION",         "year": 1739,
   "catch": "distribution shift failures"},
  {"name": "POPPERIAN FALSIFIABILITY", "year": 1934,
   "catch": "unfalsifiable AI predictions"},
  {"name": "PLATO'S CAVE",             "year": -380,
   "catch": "benchmark vs. real-world gap"},
]
for m in moves:
    print(f"{m['name']} ({m['year']}): catches {m['catch']}")

beats.narration_text: Four cells, one checklist. Each card names its philosopher, its year, its operational definition, and the AI failure it hunts.
beats.visual_intent: Manim 2x2 grid: Cartesian Doubt, Humean Induction, Popperian Falsifiability, Plato's Cave — each as a GOLD card with philosopher, move name, definition, and failure mode caught

beats.narration_text: Move one: Cartesian doubt. Inspect the source of confidence before accepting a claim. Move two: Humean induction. Training-data success does not transfer past the distribution boundary.

props.command: claude "Apply all four moves to COMPAS recidivism scoring. For each move, state the specific documented failure and the verification step that would have caught it."

beats.narration_text: Move three: Popperian falsifiability. Ask what would refute this claim. Move four: Plato's Cave. The model sees proxies — benchmarks, tokens — not the world.
beats.visual_intent: slate: COMPAS case — 4 failure modes mapped to 4 moves

beats.narration_text: The four moves convert vague doubt into a checklist with checkable answers. Each move is an engineering question.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: '4 moves -> 4 checkable engineering questions'

beats.narration_text: Apply all four to one case — COMPAS recidivism scoring — and they map directly onto four documented failure modes.
beats.visual_intent: next-steps card: run the four-move checklist on any AI output before trusting it

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [The Skeptic's Four-Move Inspection Checklist] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
